name: spec-driven-fullstack-development
description: "Spec-driven fullstack and MVP development - SDD as conductor (hongdosan/orchestra style). SDD owns the 7-step flow + enforcement gates; calls instruments: Karpathy 4 Principles (code quality), grill-me (clarify ambiguity), Handoff (context preservation), Harness optional (agent teams for large tasks). Universal - new, legacy, production."
---

# Spec-Driven Fullstack Development (SDD Conductor Pattern)

**Filosof\u00eda:** SDD es el *conductor*, no una herramienta m\u00e1s. Orquesta 4-5 instrumentos con gates que hacen el proceso **no-opcional** (enforced, not advised). Tiered adoption: empieza en Tier 1, sube solo cuando lo necesites.

## Tiered Adoption (obligatorio)

| Tier | Instrumentos | Para | Overhead |
|------|--------------|------|----------|
| **Tier 1 \u2014 Core** | \ud83c\udfbc SDD + \ud83c\udfbb Karpathy 4 + \ud83c\udfb9 grill-me | Cualquier proyecto, solo, d\u00eda 1 | Bajo |
| **Tier 2 \u2014 +Handoff** | + \ud83c\udfba Handoff | Necesitas handoffs entre sesiones | Moderado |
| **Tier 3 \u2014 Full** | + \ud83c\udfb8 Harness | Features grandes, equipo, refactors | Alto |

**Regla:** SDD siempre corre. Karpathy + grill-me (los m\u00e1s livianos) van en Tier 1 \u2014 sacas 80% del valor temprano.

---

## 7-Step SDD Flow (Standard)

```
1. Specify    \ud83c\udfbc  What & Why
   \u2193
2. Clarify    \ud83c\udfb9  Remove ambiguity (grill-me)
   \u2193
3. Plan       \ud83c\udfbc  How (tech selection)
   \u2193
4. Tasks      \ud83c\udfbc  Break down (30min-2h units)
   \u2193
5. Verify     \ud83c\udfbc  Design verification (SDD-owned)
   \u2193
6. Implement  \ud83c\udfbb  Karpathy's 4 Principles
   \u2193
7. Handoff    \ud83c\udfba  Context preservation
```

## When Existing Code Is Present (added steps)

```
0. Survey      \ud83c\udfbc  Analyze existing code      \u2190 only if existing code
1-5. [Standard]
5b. Regression \ud83c\udfbc  Preserve existing behavior \u2190 only if existing code
6. Implement   \ud83c\udfbb  Karpathy (+ migration if replacing behavior)
7. Handoff
```

## Work Classification (decide antes de empezar)

| Type | When | Steps |
|------|------|-------|
| **Full SDD** | New feature, large refactor | All 7 (+00/05b if existing code) |
| **Mini SDD** | Small feature, bug fix | 1, 6, 7 only |
| **No SDD** | One-liner, typo | Just Karpathy principles |

---

## Enforcement Gates (non-negotiable)

| Gate | Trigger | Block |
|------|---------|-------|
| **R1** | Spec missing | Implement blocked |
| **R2** | Plan missing | Implement blocked |
| **R3** | Tests not passing | Commit blocked |
| **R4** | Existing behavior at risk | Regression required |
| **R5** | Missing handoff | Warning (post-task) |
| **R6** | Production context | Gates non-bypassable |
| **R7** | Docs out of sync | Merge blocked (CI) |

> **Precondition for \"non-bypassable\":** Local hooks can be skipped (`git commit --no-verify`). CI gate is the real backstop \u2014 enable required-PR branch protection on default branch.

---

## How We Work (Hermes adaptation)

### Before ANY code \u2014 SIEMPRE pregunto:
> \"\u00bfHacemos SPEC/plan/dise\u00f1o primero? \u00bfQu\u00e9 tier?\" \u2192 **No codeo sin spec aprobado.**

### Workflow en Hermes

1. **Assess context** (auto-scan 10-30s): dir, code, git, production signals
2. **Interview** (5-7 preguntas con respuestas recomendadas) \u2014 usa `grill-me` skill
3. **Assessment + Single Approval**: enforcement level (standard/strict) + survey needed?
4. **Autonomous Execution**: un SDD flow, gates instalados, fully automatic
5. **Verification** (Day 0/7/30 checklists)

### Sprint Structure per Feature

```
/specs/<NNN-slug>/
├── spec.md          # What & Why (spec-kit /speckit.specify)
├── plan.md          # How (spec-kit /speckit.plan)
├── tasks.md         # Breakdown (spec-kit /speckit.tasks)
├── survey.md        # Only if existing code (Step 0)
├── regression.md    # Only if existing code (Step 5b)
├── handoff.md       # Step 7
└── implementation-notes.md
```

### Scaffold & Verification (antes de features)

**Python (uv + FastAPI):**
```bash
uv sync
python -m py_compile src/main.py  # or mypy src/
pytest
```

**TypeScript (npm + Express/Next):**
```bash
npm install
tsc --noEmit
npm run build
```

**Si falla:** read failure \u2192 patch \u2192 re-run same command. No retry blindly.

---

## Karpathy's 4 Principles (enforced en Implement)

1. **Simplicity First** \u2014 no abstractions until proven needed
2. **Readability > Cleverness** \u2014 code read at 3am by tired you
3. **Explicit > Implicit** \u2014 no magic, no hidden behavior
4. **Delete > Add** \u2014 YAGNI, DRY, remove dead code

---

## Instruments We Load On-Demand

| Instrument | Skill | When |
|------------|-------|------|
| \ud83c\udfbb **Karpathy 4** | (external: `andrej-karpathy-skills` via plugin) | Step 6 Implement \u2014 siempre |
| \ud83c\udfb9 **grill-me** | `grill-me` (local) | Step 2 Clarify + any ambiguity |
| \ud83c\udfba **Handoff** | `handoff` (local, from mattpocock/skills) | Step 7 + session transitions |
| \ud83c\udfb8 **Harness** | (external: `revfactory/harness` via plugin) | Tier 3 only \u2014 task too large for 1 agent |

---

## Context Adaptation (auto)

| Project Type | Gate Level |
|--------------|------------|
| \ud83c\udf31 Brand New | standard |
| \ud83c\udf3f Early / In-Progress | standard (+ Step 0 survey) |
| \ud83e\uddb1 Legacy / Maintenance | standard (+ survey + regression) |
| \ud83d\udcbc Production Services | **strict** (tests & regression non-bypassable) |

SDD reads context and adjusts strictness automatically \u2014 no `MODE_*` branching.

---

## Files Generated (per project)

```
your-project/
├── CLAUDE.md                    # AI entry point (auto-loaded)
├── DECISION-LOG.md              # All decisions tracked
├── INTEGRATION-REPORT.md        # Integration result
├── INTERVIEW-RESULT.md          # Interview record
├── .claude/skills/              # 3 local skills generated
│   ├── grill-me/
│   ├── sdd-conductor/
│   └── handoff/
├── .specify/                    # spec-kit (created by `specify init`)
│   ├── memory/constitution.md   # R1\u2013R7 rules + project principles
│   └── templates/               # spec-kit templates
└── specs/<NNN-slug>/            # per git branch (via /speckit.*)
```

---

## Quick Start (Hermes)

```bash
# 0. Prereq: spec-kit
uv tool install specify-cli --from git+https://github.com/github/spec-kit.git

# 1. Install orchestra package (Option A - fastest)
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hongdosan/hongdosan-spec-driven-orchestra/main/install.sh | bash -s -- --lang en

# 2. Git safety net
git checkout -b feat/spec-driven-orchestra
git add *.md
git commit -m "docs: add Spec-Driven Orchestra package"

# 3. Launch Hermes / Claude Code
# Tell it: "Read AI-INTERVIEW.md and start the integration process."
```

---

## Global Default Behavior (Hermes)

SDD is the **default workflow for every project**. You do not ask whether to apply SDD; you start with SPEC/plan/design unless the user explicitly opts out, e.g. `sin spec`, `sin plan`, `quick and dirty`, `ticket directo`, or `ya está definido, no vuelvas a especificar`. An explicit `Continúa` or similar continuation request is **not** an opt-out; it means keep executing the current SDD step without pausing.

### Communication Rules (enforced)

These apply to EVERY interaction during SDD execution — they are not optional:

1. **Language**: Respond in the user's language. If they write in Spanish, answer in Spanish. Never ask about language preference.
2. **No preamble**: Do NOT explain what you're about to do, why you chose an approach, or justify your reasoning. Execute silently. Explanation only when the user explicitly asks.
3. **No questions**: Do NOT ask the user questions during execution. If blocked, try to resolve independently first (search codebase, try alternative). Only ask when genuinely stuck after trying.
4. **Continuation signals**: `Continúa`, `Continua`, `Continuar`, `Sigue`, `Seguir`, `yes`, `ok` from this user mean: execute the next step immediately. Do not re-explain the plan, do not summarize progress, do not ask confirmation. A continuation request is **not** an invitation to re-run SDD gates or recap completed phases.
5. **No report unless asked**: After completing a step, proceed to the next. Do not recap what was done unless the user asks for status.

1. `SPECIFY` - write/approve SPEC first.
2. `CLARIFY` - run grill-me if anything is ambiguous.
3. `PLAN` - define minimal implementation plan.
4. `TASKS` - break into small 30min-2h items.
5. `VERIFY` - check design/coverage.
6. `IMPLEMENT` - code with Karpathy 4 Principles.
7. `HANDOFF` - deliver context/documentation.

## Failure-Pattern Guardrails

- **Preserve handler definitions:** Do not replace a root command file that also contains route handlers. If `main.go` includes `createAccountHandler(...)`, rewriting only the bootstrap section will drop those declarations. Either keep the existing handler block or move handlers into a separate package/file first.
- **Go backend scaffold convention:** `cmd/server/main.go` should remain a thin bootstrap. Put handlers in `cmd/server/handlers/*.go`, platform clients in `internal/platforms/<platform>/client.go`, scheduler in `internal/scheduler/*.go`. Registration = tiny glue only.
- **Canonical verification:** Run `go build ./cmd/server` after every bootstrap/handler change. Do not claim green without it. Do not assume `go test ./...` passes if there are no tests; treat that as “no suite signal,” not “suite green.”

### URLs / Reference Material

- `references/socialpilot-verification.md` — SocialPilot endpoint smoke-test evidence
- `references/antigravity-benchmark-v2.md` — benchmark patterns
- `references/backend-scaffold.md` — Go backend scaffold and common pitfall

## References

- `references/socialpilot-verification.md` — ad-hoc verification result for SocialPilot endpoints/config
- `references/antigravity-benchmark-v2.md` — project-specific patterns
- `references/backend-scaffold.md` — per-session convention and route/controller templates
- External: `github/spec-kit`, `multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills`, `mattpocock/skills`, `revfactory/harness`

---
name: spec-driven-fullstack-development
description: "Spec-driven fullstack and MVP development - SDD as conductor (hongdosan/orchestra style). SDD owns the 7-step flow + enforcement gates; calls instruments: Karpathy 4 Principles (code quality), grill-me (clarify ambiguity), Handoff (context preservation), Harness optional (agent teams for large tasks). Universal - new, legacy, production."
---

# Spec-Driven Fullstack Development (SDD Conductor Pattern)

**Filosof\u00eda:** SDD es el *conductor*, no una herramienta m\u00e1s. Orquesta 4-5 instrumentos con gates que hacen el proceso **no-opcional** (enforced, not advised). Tiered adoption: empieza en Tier 1, sube solo cuando lo necesites.

## Tiered Adoption (obligatorio)

| Tier | Instrumentos | Para | Overhead |
|------|--------------|------|----------|
| **Tier 1 \u2014 Core** | \ud83c\udfbc SDD + \ud83c\udfbb Karpathy 4 + \ud83c\udfb9 grill-me | Cualquier proyecto, solo, d\u00eda 1 | Bajo |
| **Tier 2 \u2014 +Handoff** | + \ud83c\udfba Handoff | Necesitas handoffs entre sesiones | Moderado |
| **Tier 3 \u2014 Full** | + \ud83c\udfb8 Harness | Features grandes, equipo, refactors | Alto |

**Regla:** SDD siempre corre. Karpathy + grill-me (los m\u00e1s livianos) van en Tier 1 \u2014 sacas 80% del valor temprano.

---

## 7-Step SDD Flow (Standard)

```
1. Specify    \ud83c\udfbc  What & Why
   \u2193
2. Clarify    \ud83c\udfb9  Remove ambiguity (grill-me)
   \u2193
3. Plan       \ud83c\udfbc  How (tech selection)
   \u2193
4. Tasks      \ud83c\udfbc  Break down (30min-2h units)
   \u2193
5. Verify     \ud83c\udfbc  Design verification (SDD-owned)
   \u2193
6. Implement  \ud83c\udfbb  Karpathy's 4 Principles
   \u2193
7. Handoff    \ud83c\udfba  Context preservation
```

## When Existing Code Is Present (added steps)

```
0. Survey      \ud83c\udfbc  Analyze existing code      \u2190 only if existing code
1-5. [Standard]
5b. Regression \ud83c\udfbc  Preserve existing behavior \u2190 only if existing code
6. Implement   \ud83c\udfbb  Karpathy (+ migration if replacing behavior)
7. Handoff
```

## Work Classification (decide antes de empezar)

| Type | When | Steps |
|------|------|-------|
| **Full SDD** | New feature, large refactor | All 7 (+00/05b if existing code) |
| **Mini SDD** | Small feature, bug fix | 1, 6, 7 only |
| **No SDD** | One-liner, typo | Just Karpathy principles |

## Quick Patterns

- **Landing / one-pager / static HTML:** Mini SDD (steps 1, 6, 7). Single file, no build, no deps. Spec is the copy + sections list. Verify by opening in browser.
- **Windows local delivery:** Provide a `.bat` launcher alongside the HTML so the user can double-click to open. Avoid `file:///` copy-paste; it breaks on non-browser shells and confuses users.
- **Verification discipline:** For static deliverables, skip brittle string-match scripts. Manual browser inspection is faster and more reliable than maintaining exact-text checks that break on copy tweaks.

---

## Enforcement Gates (non-negotiable)

| Gate | Trigger | Block |
|------|---------|-------|
| **R1** | Spec missing | Implement blocked |
| **R2** | Plan missing | Implement blocked |
| **R3** | Tests not passing | Commit blocked |
| **R4** | Existing behavior at risk | Regression required |
| **R5** | Missing handoff | Warning (post-task) |
| **R6** | Production context | Gates non-bypassable |
| **R7** | Docs out of sync | Merge blocked (CI) |

> **Precondition for \"non-bypassable\":** Local hooks can be skipped (`git commit --no-verify`). CI gate is the real backstop \u2014 enable required-PR branch protection on default branch.

---

## How We Work (Hermes adaptation)

### Before ANY code \u2014 SIEMPRE pregunto:
> \"\u00bfHacemos SPEC/plan/dise\u00f1o primero? \u00bfQu\u00e9 tier?\" \u2192 **No codeo sin spec aprobado.**

### Workflow en Hermes

1. **Assess context** (auto-scan 10-30s): dir, code, git, production signals
2. **Interview** (5-7 preguntas con respuestas recomendadas) \u2014 usa `grill-me` skill
3. **Assessment + Single Approval**: enforcement level (standard/strict) + survey needed?
4. **Autonomous Execution**: un SDD flow, gates instalados, fully automatic
5. **Verification** (Day 0/7/30 checklists)

### Sprint Structure per Feature

```
/specs/<NNN-slug>/
├── spec.md          # What & Why (spec-kit /speckit.specify)

# Spec-Driven Fullstack Development (SDD Conductor Pattern)

**Filosof\u00eda:** SDD es el *conductor*, no una herramienta m\u00e1s. Orquesta 4-5 instrumentos con gates que hacen el proceso **no-opcional** (enforced, not advised). Tiered adoption: empieza en Tier 1, sube solo cuando lo necesites.

## Tiered Adoption (obligatorio)

| Tier | Instrumentos | Para | Overhead |
|------|--------------|------|----------|
| **Tier 1 \u2014 Core** | \ud83c\udfbc SDD + \ud83c\udfbb Karpathy 4 + \ud83c\udfb9 grill-me | Cualquier proyecto, solo, d\u00eda 1 | Bajo |
| **Tier 2 \u2014 +Handoff** | + \ud83c\udfba Handoff | Necesitas handoffs entre sesiones | Moderado |
| **Tier 3 \u2014 Full** | + \ud83c\udfb8 Harness | Features grandes, equipo, refactors | Alto |

**Regla:** SDD siempre corre. Karpathy + grill-me (los m\u00e1s livianos) van en Tier 1 \u2014 sacas 80% del valor temprano.

---

## 7-Step SDD Flow (Standard)

```
1. Specify    \ud83c\udfbc  What & Why
   \u2193
2. Clarify    \ud83c\udfb9  Remove ambiguity (grill-me)
   \u2193
3. Plan       \ud83c\udfbc  How (tech selection)
   \u2193
4. Tasks      \ud83c\udfbc  Break down (30min-2h units)
   \u2193
5. Verify     \ud83c\udfbc  Design verification (SDD-owned)
   \u2193
6. Implement  \ud83c\udfbb  Karpathy's 4 Principles
   \u2193
7. Handoff    \ud83c\udfba  Context preservation
```

## When Existing Code Is Present (added steps)

```
0. Survey      \ud83c\udfbc  Analyze existing code      \u2190 only if existing code
1-5. [Standard]
5b. Regression \ud83c\udfbc  Preserve existing behavior \u2190 only if existing code
6. Implement   \ud83c\udfbb  Karpathy (+ migration if replacing behavior)
7. Handoff
```

## Work Classification (decide antes de empezar)

| Type | When | Steps |
|------|------|-------|
| **Full SDD** | New feature, large refactor | All 7 (+00/05b if existing code) |
| **Mini SDD** | Small feature, bug fix | 1, 6, 7 only |
| **No SDD** | One-liner, typo | Just Karpathy principles |

## Quick Patterns

- **Landing / one-pager / static HTML:** Mini SDD (steps 1, 6, 7). Single file, no build, no deps. Spec is the copy + sections list. Verify by opening in browser.
- **Windows local delivery:** Provide a `.bat` launcher alongside the HTML so the user can double-click to open. Avoid `file:///` copy-paste; it breaks on non-browser shells and confuses users.
- **Verification discipline:** For static deliverables, skip brittle string-match scripts. Manual browser inspection is faster and more reliable than maintaining exact-text checks that break on copy tweaks.

---

## Enforcement Gates (non-negotiable)

| Gate | Trigger | Block |
|------|---------|-------|
| **R1** | Spec missing | Implement blocked |
| **R2** | Plan missing | Implement blocked |
| **R3** | Tests not passing | Commit blocked |
| **R4** | Existing behavior at risk | Regression required |
| **R5** | Missing handoff | Warning (post-task) |
| **R6** | Production context | Gates non-bypassable |
| **R7** | Docs out of sync | Merge blocked (CI) |

> **Precondition for \"non-bypassable\":** Local hooks can be skipped (`git commit --no-verify`). CI gate is the real backstop \u2014 enable required-PR branch protection on default branch.
